Jae Woong Hwang

ADAS Controls, Embedded Validation & Vehicle Toolchain Engineer

Production ADAS controls, embedded validation, and vehicle-development toolchain experience across commercial trucks, tractors, buses, and fuel-cell platforms.

I connect production-facing behavior logic, full V-cycle validation workflows, calibration/debugging environments, and Python/C++ tooling under real vehicle-development constraints.

South Korea
jwhwang91@gmail.com
+82-10-5775-1863
Portfolio Source

Target Roles

Commercial-Vehicle ADAS SCC / L2 ADAS Embedded Validation Vehicle Dynamics Production-Facing Behavior Logic MILS/HILS Dynamometer / Real Vehicle CANape / VN1640 XCP / CAN-FD Legacy Workflow C++ / Python Tooling MATLAB / Simulink ONNX AI-Assisted Development

Professional Summary

  • Production-facing commercial-vehicle ADAS controls experience across SCC/L2 behavior logic and release-readiness support.
  • Embedded validation experience across MILS, HILS, dynamometer, and real-vehicle workflows.
  • Legacy calibration workflow experience with CANape, VN1640, XCP, and CAN-FD in constrained field-test environments.
  • AI-assisted implementation with human-owned requirements, architecture, debugging, integration, and validation.

Core Competencies

Control: Vehicle dynamics / Commercial-vehicle ADAS controls / SCC/L2 ADAS behavior logic / Front-target deceleration behavior / Target-selection robustness / Road-curvature estimation

Validation: MILS / HILS / Dynamometer / Real-vehicle validation / Calibration/debugging / Release-readiness support

Software: Python / C/C++ / MATLAB/Simulink / CANape/VN1640 / XCP over CAN-FD / ONNX / AI-assisted implementation

Professional Experience

Researcher - KAIST Master's Research Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) | 2015.03 - 2017.08
Technical Case Study

Embedded motion-control foundation: designed a controller for real turret hardware under moving-platform disturbance.

  • Designed controller for motion stabilization of a turret on a moving platform.
  • Worked across dynamics, control logic, sensor noise, and real hardware validation.
  • Built the control foundation later reused in vehicle dynamics and sensor-fusion work.

Detail: experience/kaist-masters/index.html

Researcher - TCU Application Software Agency for Defense Development (ADD), Ground Technology Research Institute | 2017.09 - 2021.03
Technical Case Study

Full V-cycle TCU productization: developed K2 55-ton tracked-vehicle TCU software from MILS/HILS to dynamometer and field validation.

  • Developed TCU application software for K2 tracked vehicle transmission control.
  • Experienced full V-cycle workflow: MILS, HILS, powerpack dynamometer, and actual vehicle field testing.
  • Worked on control algorithms, plant modeling, calibration, parameter correction, and reliability-test support.

Detail: experience/add-k2-tcu/index.html

Commercial Vehicle ADAS Controls & Validation Engineer Hyundai Motor Company (HMC) | 2021.03 - Present
Technical Case Study

Production ADAS controls, embedded validation, and vehicle-development toolchain experience across commercial trucks, tractors, buses, and fuel-cell platforms.

  • Developed and validated selected production ADAS behavior logic for commercial-vehicle SCC/L2 functions across truck, tractor, and bus platform variants.
  • Worked on SCC emergency-stop state behavior, road-radius fusion, front-target-based brake-distribution logic, and target-selection robustness.
  • Supported calibration, regression testing, vehicle-level debugging, and release-readiness activities across MILS, HILS, dynamometer, and real-vehicle validation workflows.
  • Analyzed production-development issues and supported logic/calibration updates under platform, regulation, and field-test constraints.
  • Transformed the team's existing CANape/VN1640 laptop-based calibration workflow into a minimum viable ADAS AI validation loop despite limited field-test infrastructure.

Detail: experience/hmc-adas/index.html

Selected Technical Projects

Public-safe technical case studies for validation tooling, AI-assisted implementation, and commercial-vehicle ADAS debugging workflows.

Turning a Legacy Calibration Setup into an ADAS AI Validation Loop Python / C++ / CANape / VN1640 / XCP over CAN-FD / ONNX
Technical Case Study GitHub

Constraint-driven laptop-based ADAS AI validation loop using the team's existing field calibration workflow.

Objective: Commercial-vehicle ADAS needed a practical way to explore learned-model validation on real vehicles. The mass-production ECU supported Ethernet, but Vector Ethernet interface hardware was not available in the field workflow, so I reused the available CANape/VN1640/laptop calibration setup.

Impact: Transformed the team's existing CANape/VN1640 laptop-based calibration workflow into a minimum viable ADAS AI validation loop, enabling learned-model experimentation on real production vehicles despite the lack of Ethernet interface hardware or dedicated AI compute platforms.

Python C++ XCP CAN-FD ONNX CANape/VN1640

Detail: toolchains/e2e-xcp-bypass/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/realtime-vehicle-ai-validation-studio

ADAS Log-to-AI Workbench Python / MF4 / PyTorch / ONNX / MATLAB Export
Technical Case Study GitHub

MF4 preprocessing, model training, normalization, and export workflow for ADAS engineers.

Objective: Convert CANape MF4 measurement logs into reusable AI-ready datasets and model artifacts without forcing every module owner to rewrite the same preprocessing, normalization, training, and export scripts.

Impact: Gave module owners a repeatable workflow from vehicle logs to trained model artifacts, reducing duplicated data-preparation work and making ADAS AI experiments easier to standardize across modules.

Python MF4 PyTorch ONNX MATLAB Export

Detail: toolchains/timeseries-ai-training/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/adas-log-to-ai-workbench

BEV Target Selection Validation Studio MATLAB Script / Simulink / Base Workspace / ADAS Visualization
Technical Case Study GitHub

MATLAB/Simulink replay and visualization tool for validating commercial-vehicle ADAS target-selection behavior.

Objective: Reproduce rare bus target-selection edge cases in a repeatable MATLAB/Simulink workflow instead of relying on repeated real-bus tests after every TOS/ODP logic or parameter change.

Impact: Reduced dependency on repeated vehicle tests for every iteration, helped debug false target-selection behavior, and supported more efficient logic tuning before final real-vehicle confirmation.

MATLAB Simulink ADAS BEV TOS/ODP

Detail: toolchains/tos-odp-bev-simulator/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/bev-target-selection-validation-studio
